problem.text: An even number of circles are nested, starting with radius $1$ and increasing by $1$ each time. The region between every other circle is shaded, starting with the area between radius $1$ and $2$. What is the **least number of circles** needed to make the total shaded area at least $2023\pi$?
problem.highlight: Find minimum circles where alternating ring areas sum to ≥2023π

viz_requirements.description: Concentric circles with alternating shaded rings that transform into quadratic growth visualization

actions.description: Draw a circle with specified radius
Fill the area between two consecutive circles
Display the area formula π(2k+1) next to a ring
Transform shaded rings into vertical rectangles showing area progression
Graph the function 2n² + n with horizontal line at 2023

nodes.title: Visualizing the Problem
nodes.objective: Understand the nested circles construction and shading pattern
nodes.context_from_previous: Student is familiar with area of circles but hasn't seen this alternating ring pattern

beat_outline.narration_hint: Let's start with the given example of 8 nested circles
Now we'll shade every other ring, starting between radius 1 and 2

nodes.question_hint: Which rings are shaded in our 8-circle example?
nodes.wrong_path_hint: Remember we shade every other ring starting with the first

wrong_path_acts.title: Correcting the Shading Pattern
wrong_path_acts.context_from_previous: Student incorrectly identified which rings are shaded

beat_outline.narration_hint: Let's look carefully - we shade the first ring (1-2), then skip 2-3, then shade 3-4, etc.

nodes.title: Calculating Ring Areas
nodes.objective: Derive the area formula for each shaded ring
nodes.context_from_previous: Student understands which rings are shaded but hasn't calculated their areas

beat_outline.narration_hint: The area between radius k and k+1 is π(k+1)² - πk² = π(2k + 1)
For our shaded rings (k=1,3,5,...), this gives areas of 3π, 7π, 11π,...

nodes.question_hint: What's the area between radius 5 and 6?
nodes.wrong_path_hint: Remember the formula π(2k + 1) where k is the smaller radius

wrong_path_acts.title: Reviewing Ring Area
wrong_path_acts.objective: Clarify the area between two concentric circles
wrong_path_acts.context_from_previous: Student used incorrect formula for annular area

beat_outline.narration_hint: Let's visualize why it's π(k+1)² - πk², not just π(k+1 - k)

nodes.title: Summing the Shaded Areas
nodes.objective: Develop the total shaded area formula
nodes.context_from_previous: Student knows individual ring areas but hasn't summed them

beat_outline.narration_hint: For n circles, we have n/2 shaded rings with areas following π(4m - 1) for m=1 to n/2
This sum simplifies beautifully to π(2n² + n)

nodes.title: Finding the Threshold
nodes.objective: Solve 2n² + n ≥ 2023 for the minimal n
nodes.context_from_previous: Student has the quadratic formula but needs to solve the inequality

beat_outline.narration_hint: We need to find when 2n² + n first exceeds 2023
The solution n ≈ 31.8 means we need 32 circles to exceed the threshold
